text: At the last moment, Captain Ahab arrives, angry at you for killing the white whale yourself, and stealing his moment in the sun, he knocks you unconscious with a 2x4 and kid naps you.  You awaken, in a cell in the basement of a house in 19th century Boston.  The window wells are boarded over so you can't see outside, and the door is made of bars, and you can see the sleeping guard with the keys. Around you is a butterknife, a bucket, and some loose bricks.  Someone continue...

text: This is great, and unlike most "8-bit" remixes, you didn't slur between many notes. Real 8-bit video game music never did that, and they all seem very fake when they do that. You slurred some near the end, but not as much as most 8-bit remixes.

I just have two questions:
1.) Why is this considered a "remix"?  Since it's not just the original audio with alterations, wouldn't it technically be a "cover," which would make music snobs less likely to get their panties in a bunch?

text:
Excuse me, wtf is that even supposed to mean?
I respect and love the Led probably even more than you do, and I like this because A) I like 8-bit music (and this is a good 8-bit rendition of a good song), and B) It's a tribute to what was a great song in the first place.

So why do I need professional help, now?
author: @dora_maybell

text:
No remix will ever surpass the original, that much is obvious.
Altough I don't see why people are whining so much. If you don't like this, you don't like 8-bit music at all (since it's a really good melody to whoever likes that tone), so why go listen and comment on it?

Hell, if you want to complain over slaughtering a good song, say that to the guy in "similiar videos" who made a fucking TECHNO TRANCE remix of The Stairway.
Then you have my support.

Ont: Good remix.

"LED ZEPPELIN"
